title: SSE scan_from '3 bytes back' needs explanation
description: client.rs subscribe_events: the comment says 'Advance scan_from to 3 bytes before the new data so we catch delimiters that span the old/new boundary', but does not explain WHY 3. The longest delimiter is \\r\\n\\r\\n (4 bytes), so a new reader must work out independently that only the last 3 bytes of the old buffer can form an incomplete prefix. A GitHub contributor will wonder whether 3 is off-by-one (should it be 4?). Fix: add a sentence explaining that the longest possible partial prefix straddling the boundary is 3 bytes (e.g. '\\r\\n\\r' from '\\r\\n\\r\\n'), so looking back 3 is sufficient and correct.
